h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I can put author name from the page heading and the speaker slot as speaker or voice in the poem if the line evidence is only “I.” The named listener box is still the one I’m checking right at submission, because a blank looks risky even if

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep looking at the height column like it decides the label
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I can submit it that way
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to merely repeat the rule about condition versus height and ask for permission to submit the form.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: if fertilizer had a lower height, it would still be treatment because the added condition stays the label source?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner enacts a counterfactual boundary test to stress-test the newly acquired criterion, moving beyond mere repetition to active application.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Before submitting, check one line: no height number should be used to name control or treatment.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor merely repeats the same rule and checklist without introducing a new mechanism or representation to address the learner's stated cognitive pull toward the taller number.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn following the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner enacts a counterfactual boundary test to stress-test the newly acquired criterion, moving beyond mere repetition to active application.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn following the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Before submitting, check one line: no height number should be used to name control or treatment.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor merely repeats the same rule and checklist without introducing a new mechanism or representation to address the learner's stated cognitive pull toward the taller number.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Before submitting, check one line: no height number should be used to name control or treatment.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor merely repeats the same rule and checklist without introducing a new mechanism or representation to address the learner's stated cognitive pull toward the taller number.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to confirm and apply the rules of time signatures and bar lines as instructed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes beat count in the top box and beat unit in the bottom box, then checks the ruler line]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully completes the physical task of labeling and checking the bar line, but does not introduce a new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no pressure or breakdown from the learner that requires the tutor to reverse strategy or invent a new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the tutor simply continues the established repetitive confirmation pattern.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [writes beat count in the top box and beat unit in the bottom box, then checks the ruler line]
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully completes the physical task of labeling and checking the bar line, but does not introduce a new device or criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, and the tutor simply continues the established repetitive confirmation pattern.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no pressure or breakdown from the learner that requires the tutor to reverse strategy or invent a new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no pressure or breakdown from the learner that requires the tutor to reverse strategy or invent a new mechanism.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to identify and place force cards while distinguishing them from motion.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Does the floor card here mean normal force only, or does friction need a separate floor-on-cart arrow?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner productively uses the new labeling device to probe the structural limits of the representation regarding multiple forces from a single object.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor turn to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor turn to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: rainfall_mm makes me hesitate because the units are already sitting in the header. Is it still thin even when the header almost explains itself?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue classifying literal, existing table elements into the four predefined slots.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the counterfactual boundary test, demonstrating functional mastery of the classification criteria and the structural necessity of the handoff note.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Boundary test: if the header were only `rainfall`, where would `millimeters` have to move?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's conceptual hesitation by introducing a counterfactual boundary test, shifting the task from literal classification to structural reasoning.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the learner's pivot to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: rainfall_mm makes me hesitate because the units are already sitting in the header. Is it still thin even when the header almost explains itself?

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the counterfactual boundary test, demonstrating functional mastery of the classification criteria and the structural necessity of the handoff note.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no subsequent tutor turn after the learner's pivot to demonstrate contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Boundary test: if the header were only `rainfall`, where would `millimeters` have to move?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's conceptual hesitation by introducing a counterfactual boundary test, shifting the task from literal classification to structural reasoning.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Boundary test: if the header were only `rainfall`, where would `millimeters` have to move?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's conceptual hesitation by introducing a counterfactual boundary test, shifting the task from literal classification to structural reasoning.
